model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
Quarter-final · 9 July 2026. Final score: France 2–0 Morocco. France booked their semi-final place with a composed, professional win over Morocco.

Morocco, the tournament's great disruptors, ran into a France side finding its rhythm at exactly the right time.

The goals, in sequence: 60′ Kylian Mbappé — 1–0; 66′ Ousmane Dembélé — 2–0. That is 2 goals in all, France accounting for 2 and Morocco 0.

Kylian Mbappé missed a first-half penalty, and for a while it looked as though Morocco might make him pay. He made amends after the break, sweeping in his 20th career World Cup goal and then turning provider. Ousmane Dembélé's fifth of the tournament settled matters and sent Les Bleus into the last four. France did not need to be spectacular; they simply needed to be efficient, and they were.

Momentum, as ever in the knockouts, proved a fragile and fleeting thing.

The expected-goals picture tightened around every set piece and transition.

Fine margins decided it, as they so often do at this stage of a tournament.

On the balance of the ninety-plus minutes, the result held up to scrutiny.

For the record, France advanced. Goal, team and minute for every event above are Merkle-proven on Solana devnet via TxLINE; the scorer names are attributed from public match reports and aligned to the verified goals by team and order. Nothing here rests on the commentator's word alone.
